scan.pymatgen_version_anchor: 2025.6.14 (importable in miniconda base as /Users/juicy/miniconda3/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pymatgen; MP-served docs in examples carry pymatgen_version 2024.11.13). pymatgen-analysis-diffusion and pymatgen-analysis-defects are separate add-on distributions imported under the pymatgen.analysis.* namespace; not present in base, so their APIs are anchored to AtomisticSkills usage + canonical docstrings only.
scan.role_in_software: pymatgen is the structure/analysis workhorse of the mat-* skills: it owns the crystal-structure object (Structure/Lattice/Composition), CIF/POSCAR/Vasprun/OUTCAR/LOBSTER I/O, symmetry (SpacegroupAnalyzer), and a set of pure-python physics analyses (elastic-tensor algebra, XRD kinematics, phase-diagram convex hull, diffusion Einstein relation, Wulff construction, Born/dielectric Raman post-processing). The heavy energies/forces/stresses are produced by an MLIP calculator or VASP/QE; pymatgen represents the structure they act on and post-processes the results into named physical quantities.
scan.canonical_units_note: The openmaterials map's canonical units (from omai/operator/registry.py QUANTITY_TAGS and the QE/LAMMPS scans) are the reference. Conversion factors below are quoted to full precision with the pymatgen source line that defines them.

entries.name: crystal-structure
entries.quantity: The periodic crystal: lattice vectors + species + fractional positions (the object every mat-* skill passes around)

dimension.note: container, not a single physical quantity; carries L (lattice, angstrom), N (species/counts), M (masses)

entries.units: Lattice vectors and cartesian coordinates in ANGSTROM; fractional coordinates dimensionless. Species carry Element/Species objects (atomic_mass in amu).
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.core.structure.Structure (Structure.from_file / Structure.from_str; IStructure base); pymatgen.io.ase.AseAtomsAdaptor.get_structure round-trips ASE Atoms <-> Structure
entries.conversion_to_canonical: angstrom is already the map's length unit (matches QE-scan atomic-positions after the alat/bohr conversion; matches LAMMPS metal distance).

maps_to.notes: Exact match to the opaque Phase-1 Structure node (registry tag 'structure': cell, species, positions). pymatgen is THE realization of this node across the mat-* skills; AseAtomsAdaptor is the bridge to the ASE object the MLIP calculators consume.

entries.name: cell-volume
entries.quantity: Volume of the (primitive or conventional) unit cell
entries.units: ANGSTROM^3 (Lattice.volume returns abs(det) of the cartesian lattice matrix)
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.core.lattice.Lattice.volume (via Structure.volume -> IStructure.volume)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: angstrom^3 is the map's volume unit. QE-scan CellVolume is bohr^3: 1 bohr^3 = 0.14818471 angstrom^3 (a0 = 0.529177210903 A). The mat-* skills work directly in angstrom^3, so NO bohr conversion applies here.

usage: mat-equation-of-state/scripts/calculate_eos.py:78,84,103 (equilibrium volume from MatCalc EOSCalc, reported in A^3)
mat-surface-energy/scripts/calculate_surface_energy.py:93-111 (slab a-b area from lattice; surface area is the 2D analog)
source: pymatgen/core/lattice.py:182-185 (Lattice.volume, 'Volume of the unit cell in Angstrom^3')

maps_to.notes: Direct match. Trap for cross-code identity: QE reports bohr^3, pymatgen angstrom^3. Declare units=angstrom^3 on the pymatgen realization; a compare() against a QE CellVolume needs the 0.148185 factor.

entries.name: atomic-mass
entries.quantity: Per-species / per-site atomic mass
index_structure: site index i or species index s
entries.units: amu (atomic mass units, standard atomic weights; site.mass and Element.atomic_mass return amu Mass objects)
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.core.periodic_table.Element.atomic_mass; Site.mass; Composition.weight (sum in amu)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: amu matches the map's AtomicMass unit and the QE/LAMMPS scans (QE input amu; the QE text-file 911.444 Ry-a.u. re-emission is a QE file trap, not present in pymatgen).

usage: mat-raman-spectra/scripts/analyze_raman_modes.py:226 (masses = [site.mass for site in phonon.primitive.sites]  # amu; used to mass-weight Raman displacements)

maps_to.notes: Direct match. pymatgen exposes masses in amu with no unit ambiguity.

entries.name: atom-count
entries.quantity: Number of atoms / sites in the cell
entries.units: dimensionless integer count; Composition.num_atoms is a float sum of amounts
entries.producing_api: len(Structure); Structure.num_sites; pymatgen.core.composition.Composition.num_atoms
entries.conversion_to_canonical: dimensionless; matches AtomCount.

usage: mat-surface-energy/scripts/calculate_surface_energy.py:111 (num_atoms in the slab surface-energy per-atom normalization)
mat-stability/scripts/compute_ehull.py:181 (e_above_hull is per-atom, i.e. divided by atom count implicitly by pymatgen)

maps_to.notes: Direct match (promoted parameter).

entries.name: elastic-stiffness-tensor
entries.quantity: Second-order elastic (stiffness) tensor C_{alpha,beta,gamma,delta}: second strain derivative of the energy density
index_structure: full: 4 cartesian legs (3x3x3x3); Voigt-packed: two Voigt indices 1..6 (6x6)
entries.units: pymatgen ElasticTensor is stored in eV/ANGSTROM^3 (docstring: 'in units of eV/A^3'). .voigt returns the 6x6 in eV/A^3. AtomisticSkills multiplies by EV_PER_A3_TO_GPA = 160.2176634 to report GPa.
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.elasticity.elastic.ElasticTensor (rank-4); .voigt (from pymatgen.core.tensors.Tensor.voigt, 6x6); built by matcalc.ElasticityCalc on DeformedStructureSet + stresses (ElasticTensor.from_independent_strains)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: 1 eV/A^3 = 160.21766339999996 GPa EXACTLY (pymatgen Unit('GPa').get_conversion_factor(Unit('eV ang^-3')) = 0.006241509074460764; inverse = 160.2176634). The map's ElasticConstants canonical unit is GPa. Voigt reverse-map is fixed: [[0,5,4],[5,1,3],[4,3,2]] (Tensor.get_voigt_dict / reverse_voigt_map), and Voigt has a _vscale factor of 2 on the shear off-diagonals for strain-like tensors (relevant when round-tripping compliance, not for C itself which uses vscale 1 on rows/cols).

source: pymatgen/analysis/elasticity/elastic.py:490-519 (from_independent_strains; vasp branch multiplies by -0.1 for kbar->GPa sign)

maps_to.notes: Direct match to the rank-4 ElasticConstants node (registry: 'Voigt 6x6 is a representation packing'). TWO declared conventions required: (1) unit eV/A^3 internally, GPa after the 160.2176634 factor; (2) Voigt packing with the reverse_voigt_map above. The from_independent_strains(vasp=True) path additionally does c_ij *= -0.1 to fix VASP's kbar-with-opposite-sign stress; the MLIP path used by AtomisticSkills does not, so declare stress_sign/units provenance.

entries.name: bulk-modulus-vrh
entries.quantity: Isotropic bulk modulus, Voigt-Reuss-Hill average of the elastic tensor
entries.units: eV/ANGSTROM^3 from pymatgen ElasticTensor.k_vrh; GPa after the 160.2176634 factor in AtomisticSkills
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.elasticity.elastic.ElasticTensor.k_vrh (= 0.5*(k_voigt + k_reuss)); k_voigt = voigt[:3,:3].mean()
entries.conversion_to_canonical: 1 eV/A^3 = 160.2176634 GPa (same factor as elastic tensor). Map's BulkModulus canonical unit GPa.

maps_to.notes: Direct match. matcalc reports bulk_modulus_vrh in eV/A^3; the skill converts. Declare the VRH averaging scheme (Voigt-Reuss-Hill) as provenance, distinct from k_voigt/k_reuss.

entries.name: shear-modulus-vrh
entries.quantity: Isotropic shear modulus, Voigt-Reuss-Hill average of the elastic tensor
entries.units: eV/ANGSTROM^3 from pymatgen ElasticTensor.g_vrh; GPa after 160.2176634 in AtomisticSkills
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.elasticity.elastic.ElasticTensor.g_vrh (= 0.5*(g_voigt + g_reuss))
entries.conversion_to_canonical: 1 eV/A^3 = 160.2176634 GPa. Map's ShearModulus canonical unit GPa.

maps_to.notes: Direct match. Same eV/A^3 -> GPa trap and VRH scheme provenance as bulk modulus.

entries.name: youngs-modulus-and-poisson
entries.quantity: Young's modulus E and Poisson's ratio nu, derived algebraically from K and G

dimension.note: E has stress dimension; nu is dimensionless

entries.units: AtomisticSkills computes E in GPa from K,G in GPa via E = 9KG/(3K+G); nu dimensionless via (3K-2G)/(6K+2G). (pymatgen's ElasticTensor.y_mod returns Pa via a 9.0e9 factor; AtomisticSkills does NOT use y_mod, it recomputes from GPa moduli.)
entries.producing_api: AtomisticSkills closed form (calculate_elasticity.py); pymatgen alternative pymatgen.analysis.elasticity.elastic.ElasticTensor.y_mod (returns SI Pa) and .homogeneous_poisson
entries.conversion_to_canonical: GPa; derived, not a new fundamental quantity.

maps_to.notes: No YoungsModulus or PoissonRatio node exists. Both are deterministic functions of BulkModulus and ShearModulus (E = 9KG/(3K+G), nu = (3K-2G)/(6K+2G)), so they are contract candidates downstream of the two moduli, not source-tier. Low priority: derivable. Note pymatgen's y_mod returns Pa (SI) whereas the skill's E is GPa; a naive equate is off by 1e9.

entries.name: birch-murnaghan-eos
entries.quantity: Energy-volume equation of state: equilibrium volume V0, equilibrium energy E0, and bulk modulus B0 from the Birch-Murnaghan fit

dimension.note: E0: energy (M L^2 T^-2); V0: L^3; B0: pressure (M L^-1 T^-2)

entries.units: volumes in ANGSTROM^3, energies in eV, B0 in GPa (matcalc EOSCalc key bulk_modulus_bm). The energy-volume curve E(V) is eV vs A^3.
entries.producing_api: matcalc.EOSCalc (fits pymatgen.analysis.eos.BirchMurnaghan / EOS to (V,E) from volumetric strains); pymatgen.analysis.eos.EOS
entries.conversion_to_canonical: B0 in GPa matches BulkModulus; V0 in A^3 matches CellVolume; E0 in eV matches TotalEnergy per cell (NOT per atom here).

usage: mat-equation-of-state/scripts/calculate_eos.py:71-97 (EOSCalc.calc -> bulk_modulus_bm GPa, volume A^3, energy eV, volumes/energies arrays)
source: pymatgen/analysis/eos.py (BirchMurnaghan class; matcalc wraps it) [not line-anchored: matcalc-internal fit; EOSCalc is the caller]

maps_to.notes: The EOS route grounds BulkModulus by a SECOND method (BM fit of E(V)) distinct from the elastic-tensor route; the map should carry it as an alternate producing operation. V0 -> CellVolume, E0 -> TotalEnergy (per cell). The B0 from BM and K_vrh from C_ij are an EXPECTED_AGREE pair (both bulk moduli, different algorithms).

entries.name: total-energy-per-atom-and-formation
entries.quantity: DFT/MLIP total energy, and its per-atom and formation-energy derivatives entering thermodynamic stability
entries.units: eV per cell (ComputedEntry.energy); eV/atom (energy_per_atom, formation_energy_per_atom). Explicitly PER ATOM in the phase-diagram machinery.
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.entries.computed_entries.ComputedEntry / ComputedStructureEntry (.energy, .energy_per_atom); pymatgen.analysis.phase_diagram.PhaseDiagram.get_form_energy_per_atom
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV. Map's TotalEnergy node is 'per simulation cell' (registry tag total_energy). pymatgen carries BOTH per-cell (.energy) and per-atom (.energy_per_atom); the per-atom form is the phase-diagram currency. QE-scan scf-total-energy is Ry stdout / Hartree XML: 1 Ry = 13.605693 eV, 1 Ha = 27.211386 eV.

usage: mat-defect-energy/scripts/calculate_defect_energy.py:5,218 (E_f = E_defect - E_pristine + sum dn_i mu_i, eV)

maps_to.notes: Maps to TotalEnergy but flags a UNIT TRAP the map must resolve: registry says total_energy is 'per simulation cell', yet pymatgen's stability/phase-diagram/voltage flows run in eV/ATOM (energy_per_atom, formation_energy_per_atom). Formation energy per atom is arguably a distinct node (subtracts elemental references). Recommend an explicit per_atom vs per_cell label on TotalEnergy or a separate FormationEnergy node.

entries.name: energy-above-hull
entries.quantity: Thermodynamic stability: energy of a composition above the convex-hull tie-line of competing phases
entries.units: eV/atom natively (PhaseDiagram.get_e_above_hull); AtomisticSkills multiplies by 1000 to report meV/atom
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.phase_diagram.PhaseDiagram (convex hull over ComputedEntry set); .get_e_above_hull / .get_decomp_and_e_above_hull; MP-served field energy_above_hull
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV/atom; 1000 meV = 1 eV. No node exists.

maps_to.notes: No stability/hull node on the map. High-value new source-tier node (it is the single most-used output across mat-stability, mat-phase-diagram, mat-db-mp, mat-structure-novelty). Units eV/atom (meV/atom in reports). Depends on a whole ComputedEntry set (the competing-phases convex hull), so its provenance is a SET of TotalEnergy values, not one.

entries.name: stress-tensor
entries.quantity: Cell-averaged macroscopic stress, consumed by ElasticTensor.from_independent_strains to fit C_ij
entries.units: as delivered by the calculator: MLIP/ASE stress is eV/ANGSTROM^3 (Voigt 6-vector); VASP stress is kbar with an opposite sign, which pymatgen's from_independent_strains(vasp=True) fixes via c_ij *= -0.1 (kbar->GPa and sign). The MLIP route used by AtomisticSkills feeds eV/A^3 directly.
entries.producing_api: consumed by pymatgen.analysis.elasticity.elastic.ElasticTensor.from_independent_strains(strains, stresses); produced by the ASE calculator, not pymatgen
entries.conversion_to_canonical: map's Stress canonical is a pressure tensor. 1 eV/A^3 = 160.2176634 GPa; VASP kbar = 0.1 GPa (with sign flip). QE-scan stress-tensor is Ry/bohr^3 + kbar.

usage: mat-elasticity/scripts/calculate_elasticity.py:79 (elast_calc.calc(atoms) -> stresses fitted internally by matcalc via DeformedStructureSet)

maps_to.notes: Maps to Stress. pymatgen only REPRESENTS/CONSUMES stress (in elasticity fitting); it does not compute it (the MLIP does). Record the eV/A^3 vs VASP-kbar(-sign) trap: from_independent_strains(vasp=True) applies -0.1; AtomisticSkills' MLIP path does not.

entries.name: diffusivity
entries.quantity: Self-diffusion coefficient of a mobile species from the Einstein relation on an MD trajectory
entries.units: cm^2/s (pymatgen DiffusionAnalyzer.diffusivity and diffusivity_std_dev). MSD in ANGSTROM^2, time in ps internally (time_step passed in fs).
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.diffusion.analyzer.DiffusionAnalyzer.from_structures (add-on distribution pymatgen-analysis-diffusion); .diffusivity, .diffusivity_std_dev, .msd, .dt
entries.conversion_to_canonical: map's Diffusivity node: the skill reports cm^2/s (1 cm^2/s = 1e16 A^2/s). Einstein D = MSD/(2 d t) with d spatial dims; the skill uses MSD = 6 D t (3D). LAMMPS-scan diffusivity uses the same Einstein relation from compute msd (A^2, metal ps).

usage: mat-diffusion-analysis/scripts/analyze_diffusion.py:123-135 (DiffusionAnalyzer.from_structures(..., specie, temperature, time_step_fs); D = analyzer.diffusivity in cm^2/s)
source: pymatgen-analysis-diffusion: pymatgen/analysis/diffusion/analyzer.py (DiffusionAnalyzer; not installed in base env, anchored to usage + docstring: diffusivity in cm^2/s, msd in A^2)

maps_to.notes: Direct match. UNIT TRAP: pymatgen returns cm^2/s (not A^2/ps or SI m^2/s). MSD internal unit is A^2; time_step is passed in fs but analyzer.dt is in fs and the skill converts /1000 to ps. The DiffusionAnalyzer add-on must be installed separately.

entries.name: activation-energy
entries.quantity: Arrhenius activation energy from the temperature dependence of the diffusivity
entries.units: eV (fit of ln D vs 1/T, slope * k_B). k_B = 8.617333262e-5 eV/K.
entries.producing_api: mat-diffusion-analysis/scripts/calculate_activation_energy.py (Arrhenius fit over several DiffusionAnalyzer D(T) points); pymatgen.analysis.diffusion.analyzer.get_arrhenius_plot / fit_arrhenius (add-on)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV matches ActivationEnergy. (Often reported meV or kJ/mol elsewhere; the skill keeps eV.)

source: pymatgen-analysis-diffusion: pymatgen/analysis/diffusion/analyzer.py fit_arrhenius (anchored to usage; not in base env)

maps_to.notes: Direct match (eV). Depends on a set of Diffusivity(T); provenance is multiple temperatures.

entries.name: mean-squared-displacement
entries.quantity: Mean squared displacement of the mobile species vs time (the Einstein-relation integrand)
entries.units: ANGSTROM^2 vs time in ps (analyzer.msd, analyzer.dt/1000)
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.diffusion.analyzer.DiffusionAnalyzer.msd, .dt (add-on)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: A^2 matches the map's MeanSquaredDisplacement (LAMMPS-scan msd is also A^2). NOTE the estimator difference: DiffusionAnalyzer uses multiple time origins (averaged), unlike LAMMPS compute msd (single origin) noted in the LAMMPS scan.

source: pymatgen-analysis-diffusion: analyzer.py msd property (anchored to usage)

maps_to.notes: Direct match. pymatgen's DiffusionAnalyzer MSD is multi-origin averaged (matches the catalog's definition better than LAMMPS's single-origin compute msd, per the LAMMPS scan's own EXTENSION note).

entries.name: xrd-pattern
entries.quantity: Kinematic X-ray powder diffraction pattern: peak 2theta angles, relative intensities, hkl, and d-spacings

dimension.note: 2theta: dimensionless angle (degrees); intensity: dimensionless (scaled to 100); d_hkl: L (angstrom)

entries.units: 2theta in DEGREES; d_hkl in ANGSTROM; intensities scaled so max = 100 (scaled=True); wavelength in ANGSTROM (CuKa = 1.54184 A default).
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.diffraction.xrd.XRDCalculator(wavelength, symprec); .get_pattern(structure, scaled=True, two_theta_range=(0,90)) -> DiffractionPattern with .x (2theta), .y (intensity), .hkls, .d_hkls
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node; 2theta degrees, d_hkl angstrom. Wavelength table: CuKa 1.54184, CuKa1 1.54056, CuKa2 1.54439, MoKa etc. Bragg: 2 d sin(theta) = lambda; pymatgen computes theta = asin(wavelength*g_hkl/2), s = |g_hkl|/2.

usage: mat-xrd-calculator/scripts/xrd_utils.py:1-60 (Caglioti pseudo-Voigt broadening applied downstream: FWHM^2 = U tan^2 + V tan + W)
mat-xrd-phase-analysis, mat-xrd-refinement, mat-xrd-digitizer consume the same pattern
source: pymatgen/analysis/diffraction/xrd.py:103-127 (wavelength resolution), :131-259 (get_pattern; :207 theta=asin(wavelength*g_hkl/2); :240-256 Lorentz polarization correction; :259 d_hkl = 1/g_hkl)

maps_to.notes: No diffraction node on the thermal-transport-centric map. Candidate new source-tier observable if the characterization domain is ingested; 2theta(deg)/d_hkl(angstrom)/scaled intensity. pymatgen intensity = |F_hkl|^2 * Lorentz-polarization, kinematic (no absorption/preferred orientation). Feeds mat-xrd-* refinement/matching skills.

entries.name: phonon-band-structure-and-dos
entries.quantity: Phonon dispersion omega(q,nu) and phonon density of states g(omega), as retrieved/represented from Materials Project or phonopy

dimension.note: frequency: T^-1 (represented in THz / cm^-1); DOS: states per frequency

entries.units: pymatgen PhononBandStructure/PhononDos store frequencies in THz (linear). MP DFPT reference. mat-raman converts phonopy THz -> cm^-1 via 1 THz = 33.3564 cm^-1.
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.phonon.bandstructure.PhononBandStructureSymmLine, pymatgen.phonon.dos.PhononDos (retrieved via mp_api); pymatgen.phonon.plotter.PhononBSPlotter / PhononDosPlotter
entries.conversion_to_canonical: map's Frequency tag is 'angular frequencies omega_qnu'; pymatgen/MP frequencies are THz (LINEAR nu). 1 THz = 33.35641 cm^-1; angular omega = 2pi*nu. QE-scan phonon frequencies are also linear (THz, cm^-1). The map's PhononDOS is states/cm^-1 (QE convention); pymatgen PhononDos axis is THz.

maps_to.notes: pymatgen REPRESENTS these (retrieval + plotting) rather than computing them (phonopy/DFPT do). Maps to Frequency (registry: 'angular frequencies') and PhononDOS. TRAP: registry says Frequency is ANGULAR omega; pymatgen/MP store LINEAR THz. Declare linear-vs-angular (factor 2pi) and the THz-vs-cm^-1 axis choice (33.35641 cm^-1/THz).

entries.name: force-constants-representation
entries.quantity: Real-space interatomic force constants, retrieved as an MP field (ph_force_constants) and represented by pymatgen
entries.units: MP ph_force_constants are phonopy-convention eV/ANGSTROM^2. (Contrast QE-scan flfrc Ry/bohr^2: 1 Ry/bohr^2 = 48.5829 eV/A^2.)
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.phonon.get_data_by_id(fields=['ph_force_constants']); pymatgen represents/serializes via MontyEncoder
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV/A^2 matches the map's ForceConstants[order=2] (phonopy convention). QE grounding is Ry/bohr^2 (factor 48.5829). Trap: for polar solids phonopy FORCE_CONSTANTS are the TOTAL FC2, whereas QE flfrc are short-range only (per the QE scan).

source: MP materials.phonon endpoint (mp_api); pymatgen MontyEncoder serialization

maps_to.notes: Representation-only via pymatgen (retrieval + MontyEncoder). eV/A^2 phonopy convention. Same node QE grounds in Ry/bohr^2; the phonopy total-FC vs QE short-range-FC distinction is the cross-code trap.

entries.name: born-charges-and-dielectric-raman
entries.quantity: Born effective charges Z* and clamped-ion dielectric tensor eps_inf, read from VASP OUTCAR to build Raman tensors

dimension.note: Z*: dimensionless (units of e); eps_inf: dimensionless

entries.units: Z* dimensionless (units of elementary charge e), shape (n_atoms,3,3); eps_inf dimensionless (3,3). Read via Outcar.born and Outcar.dielectric_tensor.
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.io.vasp.outputs.Outcar (.born -> Z* array, .dielectric_tensor -> eps_inf); consumed by mat-raman's Raman-tensor contraction
entries.conversion_to_canonical: dimensionless. Matches BornCharges (units of e) and DielectricTensor (dimensionless, clamped-ion). Same as QE-scan zeu/eps_inf.

usage: mat-raman-spectra/scripts/analyze_raman_modes.py:240-245 (Raman tensor: Z*_k contracted with mass-weighted mode displacement)

maps_to.notes: Representation-only via pymatgen (VASP OUTCAR parse). Z* -> BornCharges (units of e), eps_inf -> DielectricTensor (dimensionless). Same nodes QE grounds. Raman intensity itself (arbitrary units, Lorentzian-broadened) is a NEW candidate below.

entries.name: raman-intensity
entries.quantity: Non-resonant Raman scattering intensity per mode, from Born-charge-contracted mode displacements

dimension.note: arbitrary units (relative intensities), vs frequency in cm^-1

entries.units: arbitrary units (a.u.), frequency axis cm^-1; Lorentzian-broadened sum over Raman-active modes
entries.producing_api: mat-raman-spectra/scripts/analyze_raman_modes.py (compute_raman_intensities_from_born + Lorentzian simulate); uses pymatgen Outcar (Z*, eps) + phonopy eigenvectors + pymatgen SpacegroupAnalyzer point group for selection rules
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node; a.u. intensity vs cm^-1.

maps_to.notes: No spectroscopy node. Candidate new observable (a.u. vs cm^-1) if the spectroscopy domain is ingested. Downstream of BornCharges + DielectricTensor + Eigenvectors; the point-group selection rule (via SpacegroupAnalyzer) is a scheme, not a Space.

entries.name: space-group-and-symmetry
entries.quantity: Space group, point group, and symmetry-standardized cells (primitive / conventional) of a structure
entries.symbol: SG number, HM symbol, point group
entries.units: space-group number (1-230, integer), Hermann-Mauguin / point-group symbols (strings); symprec in ANGSTROM controls tolerance
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.symmetry.analyzer.SpacegroupAnalyzer (.get_space_group_symbol, .get_space_group_number, .get_point_group_symbol, .get_conventional_standard_structure, .get_primitive_standard_structure, .get_symmetry_dataset via spglib); Structure.get_space_group_info(symprec)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no numeric unit; symprec tolerance in angstrom. This is structure metadata / a representation choice, not a physical Space.

maps_to.notes: Not a physical quantity: symmetry analysis and cell standardization are representation choices ON the Structure node (which primitive/conventional cell, symprec tolerance). Belongs in representation/discretization provenance of the Structure, not a new Space. Pervasive: nearly every skill standardizes the cell before computing.

entries.name: structure-match-and-novelty
entries.quantity: Structural equivalence / similarity: whether two structures are the same phase (StructureMatcher) and the data-mined ionic-substitution probability

dimension.note: match: boolean; rmsd: L (angstrom, normalized); probability: dimensionless

entries.units: StructureMatcher tolerances ltol (fractional), stol (site-tolerance, fraction of avg free length), angle_tol (degrees); substitution probability dimensionless (data-mined lambda table, Hautier et al. 2011)
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.structure_matcher.StructureMatcher (.fit, .get_rms_dist); pymatgen.analysis.structure_prediction.substitutor.Substitutor / substitution_probability.SubstitutionProbability
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no physical node; identity/similarity metric on structures.

maps_to.notes: Equivalence relation / generative model on Structure, not a physical observable. StructureMatcher realizes the compare()/identity semantics for the Structure node (which the kernel's content-hash identity is the map-side analog of). Substitution probability is a generative prior, out of the physics-DAG scope.

entries.name: surface-energy-and-wulff
entries.quantity: Surface energy per unit area of a crystal facet, and the Wulff-construction weighted surface energy / anisotropy
entries.units: gamma computed in eV/ANGSTROM^2 then converted to J/m^2 via 1 eV/A^2 = 16.0218 J/m^2 (skill constant; exact value 16.021766). WulffShape.weighted_surface_energy inherits the input energy units.
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.core.surface.SlabGenerator / generate_all_slabs (build slabs); pymatgen.analysis.wulff.WulffShape(lattice, miller_indices, energies) (.weighted_surface_energy, .anisotropy, .shape_factor, .effective_radius). gamma itself computed in-skill: (E_slab - N*E_bulk)/(2*Area).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node. 1 eV/A^2 = 16.0217662 J/m^2 (skill uses 16.0218). Note the same eV/A^2 dimension as ForceConstants but a DIFFERENT quantity (energy per area vs force per length).

source: pymatgen/analysis/wulff.py:474 (units J/m^2 vs eV/A^2 toggle), :636-717 (volume, surface_area, weighted_surface_energy, anisotropy, effective_radius)

maps_to.notes: No surface node. Candidate new source-tier observable (J/m^2, per Miller facet) if the surface/interface domain is ingested. gamma = (E_slab - N E_bulk)/(2 Area); depends on TWO TotalEnergy values (slab + bulk) and the slab area. WulffShape aggregates per-facet gammas into a morphology.

entries.name: grain-boundary-energy
entries.quantity: Grain-boundary energy per unit area from GB and bulk energies
entries.units: J/m^2 (computed like surface energy: (E_gb - N E_bulk)/(2 A), 1 eV/A^2 = 16.0218 J/m^2)
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.core.interface.GrainBoundaryGenerator (new path) / pymatgen.analysis.gb.grain.GrainBoundaryGenerator (legacy path); GB energy computed in-skill from MLIP energies
entries.conversion_to_canonical: J/m^2; same eV/A^2 factor as surface energy.

usage: mat-grain-boundary/scripts/create_grain_boundary.py:61 (from pymatgen.analysis.gb.grain import GrainBoundaryGenerator) / :64 (pymatgen.core.interface import GrainBoundaryGenerator; version-dependent import)

maps_to.notes: Same defect-energetics family as surface energy (J/m^2). Candidate new node if the microstructure domain is ingested. pymatgen builds the GB geometry; the energy is an MLIP-energy contraction. Note the moving import path (analysis.gb.grain -> core.interface) across pymatgen versions.

entries.name: intercalation-voltage
entries.quantity: Average intercalation (electrode) voltage from de/intercalated total energies
entries.units: VOLTS. V = -(E_full - E_empty - n_ions*mu_metal)/(n_ions * z), z electrons per ion (z=1 for Li). Energies in eV, charge in e, so eV/e = V directly (no Faraday constant needed).
entries.producing_api: AtomisticSkills closed form (calculate_voltage.py); pymatgen.core Structure edits (remove_atoms.py: remove_species) prepare the de-intercalated cell. (pymatgen.apps.battery.InsertionElectrode is the library equivalent, not used here.)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node; volts. eV per elementary charge = 1 volt exactly.

maps_to.notes: No electrochemistry node. Candidate new node (volts) if the battery domain is ingested. Depends on THREE TotalEnergy values (full, empty, metal reference). pymatgen's role here is only Structure preparation; the voltage is an energy contraction.

entries.name: defect-formation-energy
entries.quantity: Point-defect formation energy from defect, pristine, and chemical-potential references
entries.units: eV. E_f = E_defect - E_pristine + sum_i(delta_n_i * mu_i), all in eV.
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.defects.generators.VacancyGenerator / SubstitutionGenerator / InterstitialGenerator (add-on pymatgen-analysis-defects) build defect Structures; E_f computed in-skill
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV; no dedicated node.

source: pymatgen-analysis-defects: pymatgen/analysis/defects/generators.py (not in base env; anchored to usage)

maps_to.notes: No defect node. Candidate new node (eV) if the defect domain is ingested. Depends on TotalEnergy(defect), TotalEnergy(pristine), and elemental chemical potentials mu_i. pymatgen (defects add-on) builds the defect geometry only.

entries.name: magnetic-moment
entries.quantity: Per-site and total magnetic moment; magnetic ordering classification

dimension.note: Bohr magnetons mu_B (dimensionless in atomic-magnetic units); ordering is a discrete label

entries.units: Bohr magnetons (mu_B) per site; total = sum in mu_B. Ordering enum via pymatgen.analysis.magnetism.Ordering (FM/AFM/FiM/NM).
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.analysis.magnetism.Ordering / CollinearMagneticStructureAnalyzer; site moments parsed from calculator output; MP field total_magnetization
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node; mu_B. total_magnetization from MP is mu_B per formula unit / per cell (MP convention).

maps_to.notes: No magnetism node. Candidate new observable (mu_B) if the magnetism domain is ingested. Ordering (FM/AFM/...) is a discrete label. pymatgen classifies/parses; the moment is a calculator output.

entries.name: band-gap-and-electronic-structure
entries.quantity: Electronic band gap, Fermi level, metal/insulator classification, and the band-structure / electronic-DOS representation
entries.units: eV (band gap, Fermi level, DOS energy axis); is_metal boolean; is_gap_direct boolean
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.electronic_structure.bandstructure.BandStructureSymmLine (.get_band_gap()['energy'], .is_metal(), .efermi); pymatgen.electronic_structure.dos.CompleteDos (.efermi, get_gap); pymatgen.io.vasp.BSVasprun/Vasprun; plotters BSPlotter/DosPlotter
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV; no map node (electronic structure is below the thermal DAG floor, analogous to the QE-scan Kohn-Sham wavefunctions being hidden).

maps_to.notes: No electronic-structure node on the (phonon/transport) map. Candidate new observable (eV) if the electronic domain is ingested; the band structure / electronic DOS are hidden electronic intermediates. Mostly representation-only (MP retrieval + plotting) in AtomisticSkills.

entries.name: cohp-bonding
entries.quantity: Crystal Orbital Hamilton Population: bonding/antibonding energy-resolved analysis (LOBSTER)

dimension.note: COHP: states-weighted energy (a.u. per bond); ICOHP: eV (integrated)

entries.units: energy axis eV; ICOHP in eV (integrated bond strength); COHP in arbitrary/relative units
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.io.lobster.Cohpcar (parses LOBSTER COHPCAR.lobster); analyzed in mat-dft-lobster
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV energy axis; no map node.

maps_to.notes: Bonding-analysis diagnostic (eV energy axis). Representation-only via pymatgen (LOBSTER file parse). Out of the thermal/mechanical DAG scope; would be a chemistry-domain node.

entries.name: ase-structure-bridge
entries.quantity: Round-trip conversion between pymatgen Structure/Molecule and ASE Atoms (the interop that lets MLIP calculators act on pymatgen structures)

dimension.note: no physical quantity; a representation adapter

entries.units: preserves angstrom lattice/positions; carries species and (optionally) magmoms, charges
entries.producing_api: pymatgen.io.ase.AseAtomsAdaptor.get_structure(atoms) / get_atoms(structure); pymatgen.io.babel.BabelMolAdaptor (molecules); pymatgen.io.cif.CifWriter (CIF export)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: identity adapter; no unit change (angstrom both sides).

maps_to.notes: Pure representation adapter on the Structure node (pymatgen <-> ASE <-> CIF). The most-used pymatgen call across the MD-based skills, because the MLIP calculators are ASE-native while pymatgen owns the structure object and its analyses. No physical content; provenance/serialization only.

open_questions: pymatgen-analysis-diffusion and pymatgen-analysis-defects are separate distributions not installed in the base miniconda env, so DiffusionAnalyzer (cm^2/s), ProbabilityDensityAnalysis, and the defect generators are anchored to AtomisticSkills usage + canonical docstrings, NOT to a read source line. A reviewer with those packages installed should confirm DiffusionAnalyzer.diffusivity units are cm^2/s and that .msd is in A^2 with .dt in fs.

TotalEnergy per-cell vs per-atom: the registry tag total_energy is defined 'per simulation cell', but pymatgen's stability / phase-diagram / voltage flows are entirely per-atom (energy_per_atom, formation_energy_per_atom, e_above_hull in eV/atom). Reviewer must decide whether to add a per_atom label to TotalEnergy or a separate FormationEnergy/EnergyAboveHull node before ingesting mat-stability.

Frequency angular-vs-linear: registry describes Frequency as 'angular frequencies omega_qnu', but pymatgen/MP PhononBandStructure and PhononDos store LINEAR THz. The factor is 2pi (angular) and 33.35641 cm^-1/THz (axis). Confirm which convention the map's Frequency node truly intends before an EXPECTED_AGREE with MP phonons.

ElasticTensor.voigt _vscale: the base Tensor.voigt applies a scale factor (2 on shear components for strain-like rank-2, and pattern for rank-4) via _vscale; verified that for the STIFFNESS C_ij the 6x6 packing used by k_voigt/g_voigt indexes voigt[:3,:3] and voigt[3:,3:] directly, so the 160.2176634 factor is the only unit conversion. A reviewer should double-check the _vscale handling if compliance (S = C^-1) is ever ingested, where the factor-2/4 on off-diagonals matters.

matcalc ElasticityCalc / EOSCalc internals: the elastic-tensor fit (DeformedStructureSet strains -> stresses -> ElasticTensor.from_independent_strains) and the BM EOS fit happen inside matcalc, not pymatgen; the vasp=True sign/kbar branch of from_independent_strains is NOT taken on the MLIP path (stresses are eV/A^3), so no -0.1 factor. Confirm matcalc does not re-apply any unit conversion before handing eV/A^3 to the skill (the skill's *160.2176634 assumes raw eV/A^3).

Surface / GB / defect / voltage energies all reduce to differences of MLIP TotalEnergy values plus geometry; whether the map wants dedicated nodes (surface_energy, grain_boundary_energy, defect_formation_energy, intercalation_voltage) or wants them as contract edges over a generic TotalEnergy + Structure is a modeling decision for the encode stage. They are grouped here as new-node-candidates with their exact closed forms.

XRD intensity model: pymatgen XRDCalculator applies the Lorentz-polarization correction and |F_hkl|^2 but NOT absorption, preferred orientation, or thermal (Debye-Waller unless debye_waller_factors passed). If mat-xrd-refinement is ingested, the intensity node needs those scheme flags declared.
GrainBoundaryGenerator import path moved between pymatgen versions (pymatgen.analysis.gb.grain -> pymatgen.core.interface); the skill try/excepts both. Pin the version when anchoring the GB node.
